payload.name: start_m
payload.description: start range in meters at which echo data is recorded. Ignored in auto range mode. Will be forced to 5% of end range as a minimum.

payload.name: end_m
payload.description: end range in meters over which echo data is recorded. Set to 0 for Omniscan 3D to automatically track the bottom and adjust range dynamically.
speed of sound, meters per second
payload.name: sos_mps

payload.description: Set to -1 for auto gain (recommended). Valid manual gain ranges are from 0 to 10
payload.name: gain_index

payload.description: pings per second = 1000 / msec_per_ping. So 100 msec_per_ping means 10 pings per second. Actual ping rate can be limited by range and speed of sound, so the value specified defines an upper limit on ping rate.
payload.name: msec_per_ping
reserved
payload.description: set to 0

payload.name: diagnostic injected signal
payload.description: must be set to 0
true to start/continue pinging, false to stop
payload.name: ping_enable

payload.description: Used by SonarView, but not necessary. If enabled log files will be much larger.
payload.name: enable_channel_data

payload.description: Must be set to false
payload.name: reserved_for_raw_data

payload.description: set to false
payload.name: reserved

payload.description: enable angle + time of flight information for each detected point should be true
payload.name: enable_atof_data

payload.description: set to 450000. Used internally for production testing only.
payload.name: target_ping_hz

payload.description: sets range resolution in number of range steps. Must be between 200 and 1000 (inclusive), 1000 is recommended.
payload.name: n_range_steps
reserved
payload.description: set to 0
Pulse length in number of range steps. Recommended value is 1.5
payload.name: pulse_len_steps

attitude_report.notes:
This data pertains to the IMU in the RX16 module. Note the 'dot' on one corner of the face of the RX16 module. The zero pitch and roll orientation is with the transducer face pointing downward with the 'dot' toward the forward/starboard corner. Roll is positive as starboard side rolls downward and port side up. Pitch is positive when the forward axis is rotated downwards.

To compute pitch and roll from the up_vec:

Pitch = asin(-up_vec.x);

Roll = atan2(up_vec.y, up_vec.z);

We may support a calibrated magnetic vector in the future, but currently a heading signal from another source is required.

payload.name: up_vec
payload.description: world up vector (x,y,z) in the device coordinate system (x forward, y port, z up).

payload.name: reserved
payload.description: reserved for future magnetic field vector

payload.name: utc_msec
payload.description: utc msec (1970 epoc). Will be 0 if not available.

payload.name: pwr_up_msec
payload.description: msec since power up

payload.name: channel_number
payload.description: assigned sequentially from 0. typically 0 for a single channel system and 0 or 1 for port/stb setup.

os3d_point_set.notes:
version: This is Version 1 of this packet format. Earlier there was a Version 0, which was applicable in some earlier beta units. These earlier files can be handled by SonarView and can be converted in SonarView as long as channel data was recorded.

power thresholds: The set of detected points is generously populated in the sense that some of these points are stronger than others and some may not be helpful. Three power thresholds are provided with which the points can be filtered if a more conservative selection of point detections is desired. 

struct atof_point_t
{
    float angle; // radians;
    float tof;   // time of flight in seconds
    float pwr;   // power level of the beam at this point
    u8 pt_type;  // 0 = unclassified, 1 = bottom_points, 2 = water column points
    u8 reserved[3] = {0,0,0};
} PACKED_STRUCT;

pt_type: At the time of this writing, all points emitted by the device will be of type 'unclassified.' In the future, we may implement further classification within the device.

Interpreting Angle

Zero angle is perpendicular to the face of the RX16 receive module. With the 'dot' end of the RX16 facing the forward direction, and the transducer side down, positive angles are to the starboard side.

Note that the angles given in the data were computed with the speed of sound also given in the data. To adjust the angle in post processing for a different speed of sound, use the Snell's Law relation.

sin(angle1) / sos1 = sin(angle2) / sos2

payload.name: ping_number
payload.description: assigned sequentially from power on

payload.name: sos_mps
payload.description: speed of sound (meters per second) used in angle calculations

payload.name: num_points
payload.description: number of points reported in the points field

payload.name: utc_msec
payload.description: ime at start of ping, UTC milliseconds (1970 epoch). Will be 0 if not available.

payload.name: pwr_up_msec
payload.description: time at start of ping from power on

payload.name: version
payload.description: 1. There was a previous version (0), see note below

payload.name: device number
payload.description: must be < number of devices (so it will be 0 for a single device)

payload.name: pwr_threshold_high
payload.description: Points with pwr higher than this are quite strong. This was the level used in all version 0 point sets. Seemed to be overly conservative.

payload.name: pwr_threshold_med
payload.description: This is a reasonable cutoff level for most applications.

payload.name: pwr_threshold_low
payload.description: This is a more liberal power level. If point_data is filtered with this threshold

payload.name: reserved[9]
payload.description: reserved for future (9 words)

payload.name: point_data[num_points]
payload.description: see above for atof_t definition

payload.name: reserved
payload.description: deprecated, may be repurposed in future

payload.name: range_start_m
payload.description: range where sampling begins

payload.name: range_end_m
payload.description: range where sampling ends
see description in ATTITUDE_REPORT
payload.name: up-vec
ping_number
payload.description: assigned sequentially from power on
-1000 if no sensor present
payload.name: water_degC

payload.description: -1000 if no sensor present
payload.name: water_bar

payload.description: 0, not yet implemented
payload.name: heave_m

payload.description: reserved for future implementation
payload.name: mag_vec

payload.description: acutal ping hz (may differ from requested)
payload.name: ping_hz_realized
gain_index
payload.description: gain index used for this ping

payload.name: pulse_usec
payload.description: length of the pulse used in this ping in usec
number of range bins processed for this ping
payload.name: n_range_bins

payload.description: number of adc samples in each range bin
payload.name: samples_per_range_bin

payload.description: must be < number of devices, so 0 for single device
payload.name: device_number

payload.description: msec since pwr up at start of ping
payload.name: pwr_up_msec

payload.description: utc msec at start of ping
payload.name: utc_msec
